name: firebase-development-add-feature
description: This skill should be used when adding features to existing Firebase projects. Triggers on "add function", "create endpoint", "new tool", "add api", "new collection", "implement", "build feature". Guides TDD workflow with test-first development, security rules, and emulator verification.
capabilities: Guide developers through 12-step workflow for adding Firebase features
Provide code templates for Cloud Functions, Firestore rules, and authentication
Enforce test-first development with unit and integration test examples
Generate consistent response patterns for API endpoints
Create Firestore security rules for server-write and client-write scenarios
author: 2389-research
securitySummary: This skill is a documentation-only guidance file for Firebase development workflows. All static findings are false positives from code examples in markdown. The skill contains TypeScript and bash code snippets as educational examples, not executable code. No actual security risks detected.
valueStatement: Adding new features to Firebase projects requires careful planning and testing. This skill guides developers through a structured workflow for adding Cloud Functions, Firestore collections, and API endpoints using test-driven development practices.
userTitle: Build Firebase Features with Test-Driven Development
limitations: Requires existing Firebase project setup, does not create new projects
Focused on JavaScript and TypeScript Firebase development only
Does not handle deployment configuration or CI/CD pipelines
Assumes familiarity with Firebase architecture and concepts

useCases.title: Add HTTP API Endpoint
useCases.description: Create a new REST API endpoint with authentication, validation, and testing for your Firebase Cloud Functions project.

useCases.title: Create Firestore Collection with Rules
useCases.description: Set up a new Firestore collection with proper security rules, indexes, and integration tests to ensure data access control.

useCases.targetUser: Mobile and web developers integrating backend logic
useCases.title: Implement Callable Function
useCases.description: Build a Firebase callable function that client SDKs can invoke with built-in authentication and structured error handling.

promptTemplates.title: Add Simple HTTP Endpoint
promptTemplates.prompt: Add a new HTTP endpoint called getUserProfile that accepts a user ID and returns profile data from Firestore
promptTemplates.scenario: Basic API endpoint creation with Firestore read operation

promptTemplates.title: Create Collection with Security
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new Firestore collection called notifications with server-write-only rules and an index on userId and createdAt
promptTemplates.scenario: Setting up secure Firestore collection with proper access control

promptTemplates.title: Build Authenticated Callable Function
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement a callable function named updateUserSettings that validates authentication and updates user preferences in Firestore
promptTemplates.scenario: Client-callable function with authentication and data validation

promptTemplates.title: Add Scheduled Background Task
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a scheduled function that runs daily at midnight to clean up expired session tokens from the database
promptTemplates.scenario: Advanced cron-based Cloud Function with database operations

bestPractices: Always write tests before implementation to ensure features work as expected
Use consistent response patterns with success, message, and optional data fields
Add security rules for every new Firestore collection to prevent unauthorized access
antiPatterns: Skipping emulator testing and deploying directly to production without verification
Writing implementation code before creating failing tests violates TDD principles
Omitting authentication checks or assuming all requests are from trusted sources

faq.question: Does this skill create new Firebase projects?
faq.answer: No, this skill is designed for adding features to existing Firebase projects. For initial project setup, use the firebase-development:project-setup skill.

faq.question: What testing framework does this skill use?
faq.answer: The skill provides examples using Vitest for unit tests and integration tests with Firebase emulators. You can adapt the patterns to other testing frameworks.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for Firebase Realtime Database?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on Firestore collections and Cloud Functions. The patterns can be adapted for Realtime Database but examples are Firestore-specific.

faq.question: How does authentication work in the generated code?
faq.answer: The skill supports both Firebase Authentication tokens and API key-based authentication. It provides templates for both patterns based on your project architecture.

faq.question: Will this skill deploy my functions automatically?
faq.answer: No, the skill guides you through development and emulator testing. You control when to deploy to production using firebase deploy commands.

faq.question: What is the standard response pattern mentioned?
faq.answer: All handlers return objects with success (boolean), message (string), and optional data fields to ensure consistent API responses across your application.
